Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria), low blood glucose and HbA1c, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, protein, glucose, bilirubin, ketones). Electrolyte disturbances (high chloride) are present. Findings suggest acute or chronic kidney disease with nephrotic-range proteinuria and possible tubular dysfunction. Urgent nephrology evaluation is recommended.
